# UC-21.8.16 · Asterisk Active Channel Saturation vs Licensed Session Ceiling

## Description

When concurrent channels approach licensed or CPU limits, new calls receive congestion tones even though individual trunks look healthy. Ingesting periodic AMI channel inventory provides a leading indicator before busy-hour meltdown.

## Value

Protects revenue and emergency call completion by triggering capacity augments or rerouting before hard blocking begins.

## Implementation

Poll AMI `Action: CoreShowChannels` and capture `ListItems` from the completing event, or run CLI `core show channels` every minute via scripted input emitting `active_channels`. Maintain a lookup `session_license_limit` per `host` (commercial Asterisk or appliance cap). Alert above 85% utilization for 10 minutes. Cross-check CPU from OS metrics.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Lookup CSV `asterisk_license_limits.csv` with host, session_license_limit.
• `inputlookup` join in SPL if not embedded in events.

Step 1 — Use `lookup` command: `| lookup asterisk_license_limits host OUTPUT session_license_limit`.

Step 2 — Validate AMI parsing against `asterisk -rx` manual count.

Step 3 — Seasonal busy-hour overlay on dashboard.

Step 4 — Escalation runbook to add SIP trunks or VMs.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip sourcetype=asterisk:ami Event="CoreShowChannelsComplete"
| lookup asterisk_license_limits host OUTPUT session_license_limit AS license_ceiling
| eval active=tonumber(ListItems), license_ceiling=tonumber(license_ceiling)
| eval util_pct=round(100*active/nullif(license_ceiling,0),2)
| where util_pct > 85
| table _time, host, active, license_ceiling, util_pct
| sort - util_pct
```

## Visualization

Gauge (util_pct), Line chart (active vs ceiling), Table (forecasted exhaust).

## References

- [Asterisk Wiki — AMI Actions (CoreShowChannels)](https://wiki.asterisk.org/wiki/display/AST/Asterisk+11+Manager+Action_CoreShowChannels)
- [Splunk Docs: Run shell scripts as scripted inputs](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/Configurescriptedinputs)
